items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

gallery.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.

thumbnail.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.

items.title: WA businesses say matter of weeks before fuel crisis sparks serious price rises

children.content: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand holding a full pint of beer below a beer tap after pouring it at a bar in a pub.

items.title: Baby killed, woman and child injured as rideshare car hits tree

children.content: A 7-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

image.altText: Lynwood crash

items.title: Cyclone Narelle devastates WA tourist town, airport 'obliterated'

children.content: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth has been left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle tore through the town.

articles.title: 'Like a war zone': Exmouth picks up the pieces in wake of ex-Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

image.altText: A tree uprootes and concrete slab distrubed at a school.

articles.title: Thousands remain without power as ex-Cyclone Narelle continues south — as it happened

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting Western Australia.

image.altText: Tin roofs collapsed on carports with a boat and car in the foreground.

articles.title: WA gas facilities, ports suffer major disruptions after cyclone

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

items.title: WA minister wants reforms to local council laws

children.content: Local Government Minister Hannah Beazley says reforms are needed to prevent local councils from quitting just a few months into the job.

image.altText: A woman is seen looking into the camera while holding a book

items.title: Remote WA area connected to outside world with first at-home internet service

children.content: Residents from remote Ngalingkadji in Western Australia's north are being connected to the internet at home for the first time, bringing banking services, telehealth and emergency services.

image.altText: a town sign with tires

items.title: EPA, developer defend records over contentious WA beach development

children.content: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

gallery.altText: An overhead shot of a beach with white sand, blue water and lots of people.

thumbnail.altText: An overhead shot of a beach with white sand, blue water and lots of people.

items.title: 'It's a nightmare': Complaints pile up over Booking.com grievances

children.content: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they've lost money, their property has been trashed and no one is taking responsibility.

image.altText: A laptop on a table with the booking.com website, a coconut drink and two logos of a palm and umbrella.

items.title: Australia has to attack misinformation about climate change, Senate report says

children.content: Australia needs to increase its efforts to fight back against misinformation about climate change, a Senate report says.

image.altText: The silhouette of an oil pumpjack in the foreground with a wind turbine in the background.

items.title: 'This cash cow is going to die': Why Chinese student numbers are falling

children.content: Chinese students continue to provide the lion's share of revenue for Australian universities. But experts warn that could soon change.

image.altText: A group of students wearing graduation gowns hold flowers.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

image.altText: Two tubes of sunscreen with 50+ SPF showing.

items.title: The 'one stop shop' providing a blueprint for Thriving Kids

children.content: While serious workforce questions surround the new Thriving Kids scheme, the organisers of an existing program in Victoria believe its model could be scaled up.

thumbnail.altText: Two toddlers interacting in a playgroup

items.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily

children.content: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a suit and glasses, standing in front an Australian flag with his hand on his stomach.

items.title: Lambs hit $500 as supply dwindles towards record low

children.content: Australian sheep and lamb prices keep rising despite global uncertainty. But how long will consumers be willing to pay?

image.altText: A pen full of lambs.

items.title: Consider yourself a trivia nut? Have a crack at this quiz

children.content: We know you've come here looking for trivia glory but you're unlikely to find any today.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains an elusive concept

children.content: The enduring challenge of finding the "perfect pair" of jeans highlights the garment's cultural significance, and the opportunity to move toward more sustainable and more thoughtfully designed denim.

image.altText: A rack of denim jeans of various shades of blue on coathangers

items.title: 'All the villages will be deserted’: The crisis emptying Greek islands and towns

children.content: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

items.title: Public transport free for Victorians in April

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Melbourne Metro city loop train

items.title: Gout beaten by Kennedy in epic Maurie Plant re-match as history repeats

children.content: In an identical performance to their titanic battle 12 months ago, Lachlan Kennedy edges out Gout Gout to win the men's 200 metres at the Maurie Plant Meet in Melbourne.

image.altText: Lachlan Kennedy defeats Gout Gout in the men's 200m at the 2026 Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: The unexpected type of man more women want to date

children.content: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

items.title: Teenage cricketer Mia Barwick tipped for cricketing stardom

children.content: Mia Barwick is just 15 and a multi-sport prodigy, bound for cricketing glory. She's part of the next generation of female sporting stars — but with that comes pressure of the spotlight.

items.title: Former AFL footballer asked how much he drank before he was allegedly raped

children.content: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

image.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.
